metadata.summary: Two core PM pipelines: Project Bootstrap (idea → constitution → TASK_000) and MLP Planning (MVP → Most Lovable Product) enforcing required sequencing and artifacts.

content.title: Product Manager Workflows
content.summary: Two core PM pipelines: Project Bootstrap (idea → constitution → TASK_000) and MLP Planning (MVP → Most Lovable Product). Bootstrap is interactive and requires explicit user confirmation at Phase 4; MLP runs five `faion-mlp-agent` modes sequentially. Both pipelines treat `.aidocs/features/` as the planning source of truth and Jira/Linear as the execution mirror — never the reverse.

why.p: Without a structured bootstrap pipeline, projects start without a constitution, backlog items lack FR/AC numbering, and RICE scores are never assigned. The MLP transition is where "minimum viable" products become products people love — but only when grounded in real competitor evidence and telemetry, not speculation. These workflows enforce the required sequencing and artifacts.

list.item: New project bootstrap where `.aidocs/` is empty and `constitution.md` + `roadmap.md` need authoring before any code task.
MVP-to-MLP transition after first usable build ships and produces real telemetry.
Sprint kickoff and review ceremonies where backlog needs grooming, sizing, and tracker sync.
Release coordination: changelog generation, release-notes draft, GTM handoff.
Daily PM ritual: backlog re-prioritization, stakeholder digest, blocker surfacing.
During SDD task execution itself — code tasks belong to `feature-executor`. PM workflow stops at TASK_000.
One-off feature requests inside an active sprint — log to backlog via tracker API.
Spec changes after spec freeze — route through change management.
Solo Phase 1 before anything to prioritize — use `solo/product/product-planning/mvp-scoping` instead.

section.name: bootstrap-pipeline
section.p: Seven-phase pipeline from idea to TASK_000, with mandatory Phase 4 user confirmation and the output artifact structure under .aidocs/.

Phase sequence: 1. Vision Brainstorm (Five Whys to real need) → 2. Tech Stack Selection (frontend, backend, database) → 3. MVP Definition (max 3-5 features, cut 50% nice-to-haves) → 4. User Confirmation (MANDATORY — present summary, get explicit approval) → 5. Backlog Creation (feature specs with FR/AC) → 6. Constitution (write project constitution) → 7. TASK_000 (setup task with project goals).
Bootstrap produces: .aidocs/constitution.md, .aidocs/roadmap.md, .aidocs/features/backlog/{NN}-{feature}/spec.md (max 5 features), .aidocs/features/00-setup/tasks/todo/TASK_000_project_setup.md.

rule.statement: Phase 4 user confirmation cannot be skipped or simulated — the bootstrap pipeline must hard-fail with NEEDS-HUMAN if no explicit approval token is received.
rule.rationale: LLMs eager to complete will simulate user approval; bootstrap must detect non-interactive mode and exit rather than invent answers. An unsigned constitution is not a constitution.

rule.statement: Bootstrap must detect non-interactive mode (cron, CI) and exit with FAIL needs-human; do not invent answers.
rule.rationale: The AskUserQuestion step blocks in non-interactive runs; an agent that invents user approval produces a constitution nobody agreed to, corrupting the entire project record.

rule.statement: Every spec must have a RICE row (reach/impact/confidence/effort frontmatter) before leaving backlog; reject specs without RICE at grooming time.
rule.rationale: Unscored backlog items cannot be prioritized; RICE without frontmatter is invisible to the rice-reorder script and the sprint kickoff agent.

rule.statement: Agents must refuse to invent features not present in the idea-validation or competitive-analysis inputs.
rule.rationale: MLP hallucination — inventing WOW features without competitor evidence — is the most common failure mode; the PM's job is to make evidence the precondition, not the review step.

rule.statement: When two PM agents may run concurrently, take a flock on .aidocs/.numbering.lock before assigning the next feature number or task number.
rule.rationale: Concurrent agents assigning {NN} without a lock create numbering collisions that break spec filenames and all downstream references.

section.name: mlp-planning-workflow
section.p: Five-mode sequential MLP pipeline that transforms a shipped MVP into a Most Lovable Product, with constraints on WOW feature grounding and a cap of 7 WOW items.

rule.statement: Run the five faion-mlp-agent modes strictly in sequence: analyze → find-gaps → propose → update → plan. Each mode persists its artifact so a failed run can resume from the last completed mode.
rule.rationale: Skipping modes — especially running propose before analyze — produces WOW features ungrounded in competitor evidence, which is the primary MLP failure mode.

section.p: Output artifacts: product_docs/mvp-scope-analysis.md (analyze), product_docs/mlp-analysis-report.md (find-gaps), product_docs/mlp-implementation-order.md (plan). Each file is the input to the next mode.

rule.statement: Every WOW feature must cite a row in mvp-scope-analysis.md; features with no competitor evidence are labeled "speculative" and placed under "Deferred — needs validation".
rule.rationale: Speculative WOW features not grounded in competitor evidence or customer data are guesses; they belong in the deferred bucket, not the MLP roadmap.

rule.statement: Cap WOW features at 7; more than 7 and the MLP becomes a v2.0 plan, not an MLP.
rule.rationale: MLP is the minimum set of WOW features that makes users love the product — a superset of MVP but not a full feature rebuild. Empirically, 8+ WOW features require a second release cycle to ship.

rule.statement: Run MLP propose only after the MVP ships and produces real telemetry; propose-without-data is the primary failure mode the workflow does not warn about in its README.
rule.rationale: WOW feature selection without usage data is marketing speculation; the propose mode needs to know which MVP features users actually used before proposing enhancements.

antipattern.description: MLP "update" mode rewrites spec files in-place on retry, clobbering FR numbering.
antipattern.reason: Always run git status before and after update mode; agents on retry have been observed overwriting FR-NN.N numbering with duplicates. Use the artifact files as checkpoints, not as editable scratch space.

antipattern.description: Running the full MLP pipeline from cron when the product is still in MVP phase with no telemetry.
antipattern.reason: MLP propose generates fictional WOW features when there is no usage data; the cron trigger must check for the existence of a telemetry artifact before running.

section.name: numbering-conventions
section.p: Standard numbering patterns for features, tasks, requirements, and acceptance criteria used across the PM workflow artifacts.

rule.statement: Use these exact patterns across all PM workflow artifacts: features as {NN}-{name} (e.g., 01-auth), tasks as TASK_{NNN}_* (e.g., TASK_001_setup), functional requirements as FR-{NN}.{N} (e.g., FR-01.1), acceptance criteria as AC-{NN}.{N} (e.g., AC-01.1).
rule.rationale: Consistent numbering enables cross-referencing between specs, task files, and tracker tickets; inconsistent numbering breaks the rice-reorder script, spec-to-task linking, and MLP update mode's in-place edit logic.

rule.statement: Treat .aidocs/features/ as the planning source of truth and Jira/Linear as the execution mirror — never the reverse.
rule.rationale: Tracker tickets get deleted, re-numbered, or migrated; the .aidocs/ file tree is under git and has full history. If the tracker is the source of truth, history is lost when tickets are deleted.

rule.statement: When syncing RICE values to Jira numeric custom fields, coerce explicitly; Jira drops the value silently on type mismatch.
rule.rationale: Jira's REST API does not return an error when a numeric field receives a string value; the value is silently ignored, leaving the field empty in the tracker while appearing correct in the spec frontmatter.

rule.statement: The daily PM orchestrator produces one digest file (.aidocs/digest/YYYY-MM-DD.md) and sends only the diff vs. yesterday via tg-send, not the full digest.
rule.rationale: Sending the full digest daily creates Telegram noise; the diff is what the PM needs to act on. Full digests are available on demand from the file.

section.name: orchestration-and-cadence
section.p: Drive the PM cadence as a **daily orchestrator** (cron + on-demand) plus three event-driven flows: **bootstrap**, **sprint**, **release**. The orchestrator wakes, reads `.aidocs/features/`, syncs deltas to the tracker, and emits a digest. Sprint and release flows are explicit `Task` invocations from the user. Never run bootstrap from cron — it is interactive (Phase 4 confirmation is mandatory). For MLP, sequence the five `faion-mlp-agent` modes strictly (`analyze → find-gaps → propose → update → plan`); each mode persists its artefact so a failed run resumes.

list.item: `faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent` — Pulls competitor MVP scope; required input for Phase 3 of bootstrap and Phase 1 of MLP.
`faion-mlp-agent` — Five-mode orchestrator (`analyze`, `find-gaps`, `propose`, `update`, `plan`). Each mode = one Task invocation.
`faion-sdd-executor-agent` (in `agents/`) — Downstream of TASK_000 creation; receives the first executable task.
`faion-marketing-manager` — Downstream of release flow; consumes `executive-summary.md` plus release-notes draft for GTM.
`faion-business-analyst` — Required between Phase 5 (backlog) and Phase 6 (constitution) for FR/AC formalisation.
General-purpose `Task` subagent — Use for backlog grooming + tracker sync when no specialised PM-ops agent is registered.

section.name: prompt-patterns
section.prompt: You are the PM orchestrator. Inputs: .aidocs/product_docs/idea-validation.md .aidocs/product_docs/competitive-analysis.md For each MVP feature (max 5), create: .aidocs/features/backlog/{NN}-{slug}/spec.md with sections: User Story, FR-NN.N (max 5), AC-NN.N (Given/When/Then), RICE score, MoSCoW tag. Refuse to invent features not present in inputs. Return: list of created paths, RICE table, blocking questions.

mode=sprint-kickoff sprint_id={N} Steps: 1. Read .aidocs/features/backlog/, sort by RICE desc 2. Pull next K features whose sum(effort) <= team_capacity_pd 3. Move backlog/ → todo/, generate TASK_NNN_*.md per feature 4. Sync to Jira/Linear via MCP; attach spec.md as description Output: sprint plan as table, list of skipped features with reason.

mode=propose project_path=. Constraints: every WOW feature must cite a row in mvp-scope-analysis.md. If no competitor evidence exists for a feature, label it "speculative" and place under "Deferred — needs validation". Max 7 WOW items.

section.name: limitations-and-failures
section.p: **Tracker drift.** Workflow writes to `.aidocs/features/backlog/` but Jira/Linear are the source of truth for many teams. Without a sync subagent, the two diverge within one sprint.

**No stakeholder loop.** Bootstrap Phase 4 is a single user confirmation; real PM workflows require sign-off from eng lead, design, and exec sponsor. Agent skips this and produces unsigned constitutions.
**MLP "WOW" generation hallucinates.** `faion-mlp-agent` mode=`propose` invents features without competitor evidence unless explicitly grounded in `mvp-scope-analysis.md`.
**Numbering collisions.** `{NN}-{feature}` and `TASK_{NNN}` numbering breaks when two parallel agents create features simultaneously — no lock file in current spec.
**No definition-of-ready / definition-of-done gates.** Tasks land in `todo/` whether or not the spec passes INVEST. Add a validator subagent.
**Release management absent.** README does not cover release branching, RC tagging, or rollback windows. Use `pro/product/product-manager/release-planning` alongside.
**Capacity blindness.** RICE Effort assumes a homogeneous team; agent has no model of available developer-days, so prioritisation drift is common.

section.name: cli-tools
section.p: gh project — Read/write GitHub Projects v2 fields, issues, PRs — `gh extension install github/gh-projects` · https://cli.github.com/manual/gh_project
jira-cli (ankitpokhrel) — Headless Jira ops: issue create, transition, JQL search — `go install github.com/ankitpokhrel/jira-cli/cmd/jira@latest`
linear-cli (community) — Linear teams/issues from terminal — `npm i -g @evangodon/linear-cli`
shortcut-cli — Shortcut (Clubhouse) story CRUD — `npm i -g shortcut-cli`
notion-cli (litencatt) — Notion DB read/write for roadmap pages — `brew install litencatt/tap/notion-cli`
productboard-cli (community) — Productboard feature ingest — https://www.npmjs.com/package/productboard-api
git-changelog-command-line — Auto-changelog by conventional commits — `npm i -g conventional-changelog-cli`
release-please (Google) — Release PRs from conventional commits — `npm i -g release-please`
semver-cli — Validate / bump semver in release flow — `npm i -g semver`
dust (renamed `dst`) — RICE/MoSCoW spreadsheet via CSV — local Python script

section.name: services-and-apps
section.p: Jira Cloud (SaaS) — Use Atlassian Remote MCP or `jira-cli`; rate limit 100 req/min/user. — Yes — REST + JQL + MCP server
Linear (SaaS) — Best-in-class API; webhooks for sprint events. — Yes — GraphQL + official MCP
Shortcut (SaaS) — Story `external_id` field for two-way sync. — Yes — REST
GitHub Projects v2 (SaaS) — Use `gh` CLI; fields are typed (single-select, iteration). — Yes — GraphQL
Productboard (SaaS) — Good for feedback ingest; portal not scriptable. — Partial — REST, no MCP
Aha! (SaaS) — Heavy enterprise; PMs use for portfolio, not sprint. — Partial — REST
Notion (SaaS) — Roadmap source-of-truth for solo/small teams. — Yes — REST + MCP
ClickUp (SaaS) — Watch rate limits; many fields are custom. — Yes — REST
OpenProject (OSS, self-hosted) — Run on faion-net server; Docker compose. — Yes — REST
Plane (makeplane) (OSS, self-hosted) — Modern Linear-alternative; active dev. — Yes — REST
Taiga (OSS) — Scrum + Kanban; smaller community. — Yes — REST
Focalboard (OSS) — Local-only kanban. — Limited — API closed since Mattermost merge
LaunchDarkly (SaaS) — Feature flag gates for release management. — Yes — REST
Statsig (SaaS) — Experimentation + flags; PM owns config. — Yes — REST

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Treat `.aidocs/features/` as the planning source of truth and Jira/Linear as the **execution mirror** — never the reverse, or agents lose history when tickets are deleted.
Lock numbering. When two PM agents may run concurrently, take a flock on `.aidocs/.numbering.lock` before assigning the next `{NN}` or `TASK_{NNN}`.
Every spec must have a RICE row before it leaves backlog; reject specs without `reach/impact/confidence/effort` frontmatter at grooming time.
Run MLP propose only after MVP ships and produces real telemetry — propose-without-data is the failure mode README does not warn about.
Cap WOW features at 7. Empirically, more than that and the MLP becomes a v2.0 plan, not an MLP.
Daily orchestrator should produce **one** digest file (`.aidocs/digest/YYYY-MM-DD.md`) and `tg-send` only the diff vs yesterday — saves Telegram noise.
Hold a `release-cut` ceremony agent that creates the release branch, generates changelog, opens the PR, and pings the GTM agent — don't bolt this onto sprint kickoff.

section.name: ai-agent-gotchas
section.p: **Phase 4 confirmation cannot be skipped.** LLMs eager to finish will simulate user approval. Hard-fail the flow if the user-message stream lacks an explicit `approve` token.
**`AskUserQuestion` blocks in non-interactive runs** (cron, CI). Bootstrap must detect non-interactive mode and exit with `FAIL needs-human` — do not invent answers.
**Specs are markdown, trackers are typed.** When syncing RICE values to Jira numeric custom fields, coerce explicitly; otherwise Jira drops the value silently.
**MLP "update" mode rewrites spec files in-place.** Always run `git status` before and after — agents have been observed clobbering FR numbering on retry.

**Tracker MCP token leakage.** Linear and Jira MCP servers cache OAuth tokens; in a worktree-isolated agent, the token may belong to a different user. Verify `whoami` against the tracker before write ops.

**Capacity field is unreliable.** Sprint kickoff agent should require explicit `team_capacity_pd` argument from user; never compute from velocity alone (velocity is a trailing indicator and breaks on holiday weeks).
**Hidden features.** PMs sometimes file features as `epic` in the tracker but as `feature/` in `.aidocs/`. The sync agent must understand both shapes; otherwise epics duplicate.
**No DoD validator.** Add a `definition-of-done.yaml` per feature and a checker subagent — without it, `done/` collects half-merged work.

section.name: references
section.p: Reforge — "PM Operating Model" (sprint cadence + ceremonies): https://www.reforge.com/blog
Marty Cagan — Inspired (Phase 4 confirmation philosophy): SVPG, 2017.
Atlassian — Jira Cloud REST v3 + JQL: https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v3/
Linear — API + MCP: https://developers.linear.app/
GitHub Projects v2 GraphQL: https://docs.github.com/en/graphql/reference/objects#projectv2
release-please (Google): https://github.com/googleapis/release-please
Companion methodology: `pro/research/researcher/workflows` (research-side state machine)
Companion methodology: `pro/pm/pm-agile/scrum-ceremonies` (sprint mechanics this workflow drives)

template.purpose: Rank backlog by RICE score, write sorted INDEX.md
